text.title: Regulatory Compliance 2026 Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure to produce a defensible 2026 accessibility compliance report from surface inventory to published statement.

step.name: inventory-surfaces
step.input: Product catalog, GTM market list, app-store metadata.
step.action: List every public surface (web URLs, native bundle IDs, kiosk firmware, e-book ISBNs). For each, mark active jurisdictions (US, EU, ON, US-FED, UK, AU, JP). Output: `surfaces[]` array.
step.decision-gate: If <1 surface, this methodology does not apply — stop.

step.name: map-to-regulations
step.action: For each (surface, jurisdiction) pair, attach the binding regulation (ADA Title II / EAA / AODA / Section-508 / UK-EQA / AU-DDA), required WCAG version + level, and effective deadline. Output: `regulations[]` array.
step.decision-gate: If a surface has no regulation row in any active jurisdiction, note "non-public-accommodation" justification; never silently omit.

step.name: run-conformance-testing
step.action: Run axe / pa11y / Lighthouse on every changed page (automated). Run manual keyboard + screen-reader sweep on critical journeys. Schedule at-least-one AT-user session per major release. Record results.
step.decision-gate: If AT-user session missing this release cycle, downgrade conformance claim from "conformant" to "partial conformance with named gaps".

step.name: draft-statement
step.action: Fill `templates/accessibility-statement.md`: WCAG version + level (use 2.2 AA as design baseline), methodology checkboxes (automated / manual / at_user), feedback channels (≥1 email + 1 voice or form), remediation commitment timeline, last-updated date.

step.name: legal-review
step.input: Draft statement + testing log + regulation matrix.
step.action: Route to counsel. Counsel checks for over-statement, missing jurisdictions, overlay-mention, and remediation-commitment realism. Sign-off recorded against `report_date`.
step.decision-gate: If counsel refuses sign-off, return to step 3 or 4 and tighten the claim.

step.name: publish-and-schedule-review
step.action: Publish to public URL. Run `scripts/validate-regulatory-compliance-2026.py` against the JSON report — must exit 0. Schedule next review (quarterly + per major release). File backlog items in tracker with WCAG SC IDs.
step.output: Statement live; backlog tracked; calendar scheduled.
